title: God and Human Beings in the Technologically-Mediated Living Condition: Philip K. Dick's Literary Theology in the VALIS Trilogy
author: Laurie Jui-hua Tseng
card_summary: An academic article exploring Philip K. Dick's literary theology in the VALIS trilogy, arguing that Dick portrays the technologically-mediated human condition as a simulacrum of God's divine matrix. Examines the concepts of God as Zebra, the distinction between divine and technological systems through love and living presence, and Dick's concealment of his Gnostic leanings.
